behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator -> row-index tracking -> DLQ writer handoff, not a single pure function. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of schema validation alone can pass while row-number propagation to DLQ is wrong due to module wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; an in-process fake DLQ sink is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: an Integration test can catch an off-by-one row number introduced by CSV parser batching that a Unit test of `validateRow` would miss. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real network/OS effects; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline modules together on a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields; assert invalid rows are emitted to DLQ payload including exact original CSV row numbers (including header offset rules).

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is transformation logic for parsing/normalizing date formats; failure is inside one function/object boundary. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module dependency is needed to reproduce parsing ambiguity if inputs are passed directly. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF for higher levels is not applicable because Unit already targets the true boundary. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System rejected because behavior text is only about conversion correctness, not inter-module handoff or infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterized unit tests over representative ISO 8601, US (MM/DD/YYYY), and European (DD/MM/YYYY) strings (plus edge cases) asserting one canonical output format and explicit failures for ambiguous/invalid dates.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on runtime memory pressure, allocator behavior, and wall-clock throughput under realistic process conditions. MOCK-MISS: in-process mocks for I/O often remove backpressure/serialization costs and hide OOM/timeout failures. REAL-INFRA: requires real process and OS memory limits/timing characteristics, so System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with stubbed readers/writers may pass while production process exceeds memory due to actual buffering and GC behavior on large streams.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real destination dependencies used in non-prod env; enforce timeout and memory ceiling, and assert successful completion without OOM/kill.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is retry orchestration around an external client call; correctness is in module interaction (reader + retry policy + batch controller). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing backoff math alone can miss that batch controller incorrectly aborts on first 503. REAL-INFRA: real network is not strictly required if a controllable S3 client stub returns 503 then success and a fake clock verifies backoff schedule. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit tests of backoff function can pass while integration still fails because exception mapping from S3 client is wrong, so no retry occurs. REJECTION-GATE: System considered but rejected because behavior text does not require proving socket/HTTP stack behavior, only pipeline retry semantics on 503 responses.
behaviors.test_description: Wire pipeline reader, retry policy, and batch orchestration with an in-process S3 stub that emits 503,503,200; assert retries occur with expected exponential delays and batch continues after recovery.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary includes crash/restart behavior plus durable DB state across process lifecycles. MOCK-MISS: graceful in-process restarts or mocked repos can hide duplicate writes caused by real crash timing and transaction boundaries. REAL-INFRA: requires real process restart semantics (e.g., kill/restart) and real PostgreSQL persistence behavior, so System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration tests that simulate restart by re-calling functions may miss duplicates that appear only when process is terminated after commit but before checkpoint/offset flush.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against real PostgreSQL, force mid-batch process termination (non-graceful), restart from persisted checkpoint/offset, then assert target table contains exactly-once rows (no duplicates) for already committed records.
